question_title: Will any model evaluated by Epoch AI score at least 40.0% on FrontierMath Tier 4 before May 1, 2026?
summary: The current FrontierMath Tier 4 record is 38% (18/48) by GPT-5.4 Pro, set March 5, 2026. Reaching 40.0% requires solving at least 20/48 problems (41.67%). With only 19 days until the May 1 deadline, the question hinges on whether a new model (most likely GPT-5.5 "Spud", whose pre-training completed March 24) will be evaluated by Epoch AI on Tier 4 in time. The pass@10 for GPT-5.4 was also 38%, suggesting the current model family has plateaued. While the AI capability frontier is advancing rapidly, the specific bottleneck is Epoch AI's evaluation process - no new Tier 4 evaluation has been published since March 5. The most plausible YES path requires GPT-5.5 post-training to complete by ~April 25 AND Epoch AI to evaluate it promptly, both of which are uncertain.

factors.description: Current record is 38% (18/48), threshold requires 20/48 (41.67%) - need 2 more problems solved in a single run
Pass@10 for GPT-5.4 (xhigh) was also 38%, suggesting current architecture plateau at this level
GPT-5.5 'Spud' pre-training completed March 24, still in post-training - could be ready by late April but timeline is very tight
Only 19 days remain until resolution deadline (May 1, 2026)
No new Tier 4 evaluation has been published since March 5 (38 days ago)
Epoch AI has pre-release access agreements with OpenAI and evaluates new models quickly
Score progression shows rapid improvement: 2% to 19% to 31% to 38% in ~8 months, though jumps are diminishing (17pp to 12pp to 7pp)
20/48 problems already solved across all models ever (pass@kitchen-sink = 42%), so the problems ARE solvable
Claude Mythos Preview released April 7 but Epoch AI has never evaluated Claude models on Tier 4
Epoch AI primarily evaluates OpenAI models on Tier 4 (funded by OpenAI), limiting candidate models

reasoning:
**Forecast: 18% probability (logit: -1.5)**

Key reasoning:

- **Current state**: GPT-5.4 Pro holds the record at 38% (18/48 problems). Reaching 40.0% requires 20/48 (41.67%), meaning a model must solve 2 more problems than the current best in a single run.
- **Plateau signal**: Even with 10 attempts (pass@10), GPT-5.4 couldn't break past 38%, suggesting the current model generation has hit a ceiling.
- **Timeline is the binding constraint**: Only 19 days remain. The most likely candidate (GPT-5.5 "Spud") completed pre-training March 24 and is still in post-training. Whether it can be ready, evaluated by Epoch AI on Tier 4, and results published before May 1 is highly uncertain.
- **Evaluation bottleneck**: Epoch AI must conduct the evaluation — no other source counts. They've only done ~4-5 Tier 4 evaluations ever, and none since March 5.
- **IF a next-gen model IS evaluated**, the trend (2%→19%→31%→38%) suggests it could plausibly reach 40%+, but the probability of the evaluation happening in time is the dominant uncertainty.
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will any model evaluated by Epoch AI score at least 40.0% on FrontierMath Tier 4 before May 1, 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/43134)
[FRONTIERMATH: A BENCHMARK FOR EVALUATING ADVANCED MATHEMATICAL REASONING IN AI](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2411.04872)
[(Manifold) What will be the best FrontierMath Tier 4 score by Dec 31, 2026?](https://manifold.markets/Bayesian/what-will-be-the-best-frontiermath)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves Yes if Epoch AI publicly reports a FrontierMath Tier 4 accuracy score of 40.0% or higher for any model before May 1, 2026. Otherwise it resolves No. According to the Leaderboard here: [https://epoch.ai/benchmarks/frontiermath-tier-4/](https://epoch.ai/benchmarks/frontiermath-tier-4/)
